input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use an MSBuild condition on `IsTestProject`, which MSTest sets to `true` automatically when you reference `MSTest`:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <!-- shared props for all projects -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** The `MSTest` NuGet package (v3.x+) sets `IsTestProject=true` during evaluation. Library/production projects never reference it, so the condition is `false` for them.

**Alternative** if you use a naming convention: you could match on `$(MSBuildProjectName).EndsWith('.Tests')`, but `IsTestProject` is the idiomatic, package-driven approach.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question — no codebase exploration needed.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the MSTest package reference or a convention like the project name ending in `.Tests`. But the cleanest MTP-specific approach is to condition on `IsTestProject`.

Let me give a concise answer.
